21.correct: The President of India decides the number of judges in a High Court (the President appoints them and fixes the strength).

wrong.B: Parliament can regulate by law but the strength is determined by the President.
wrong.C: The Chief Justice of India is consulted but does not fix the number.
wrong.D: The Governor does not decide the number of High Court judges.

21.fact: High Court judges are appointed by the President under Article 217.

22.correct: The Fazl Ali Commission (States Reorganisation Commission, 1953) recommended reorganising states largely on a linguistic basis.

wrong.A: The Rajamannar Commission dealt with Centre-State relations.
wrong.C: The Punchhi Commission examined Centre-State relations later.
wrong.D: The Sarkaria Commission also dealt with Centre-State relations.

22.fact: The States Reorganisation Act, 1956 redrew state boundaries along linguistic lines.

23.correct: Bhangra originated in the Punjab region.
23.fact: Bhangra is traditionally performed during the Baisakhi harvest festival.

24.correct: Action and reaction forces are equal in magnitude but produce different accelerations because the two objects have different masses (a = F/m).

wrong.A: The forces act on different objects, not the same one.
wrong.B: The forces are in opposite directions.
wrong.D: The forces are equal in magnitude, not unequal.

24.fact: This follows from Newton's third law combined with the second law (a = F/m).

25.correct: 'Maila Aanchal' is a famous Hindi novel by Phanishwar Nath Renu.
25.fact: 'Maila Aanchal' (1954) is a landmark regional (anchalik) novel set in rural Bihar.

26.correct: Pullela Gopichand won the All-England Open Badminton Championships in 2001.
26.fact: Gopichand was the second Indian to win the prestigious All-England title (after Prakash Padukone).

27.correct: The Third Five-Year Plan (1961-66) is regarded as a failure because wars (with China in 1962 and Pakistan in 1965) and droughts forced India into food and import dependence.

wrong.B: Industry did not simply grow as planned; the plan was disrupted.

27.fact: After the Third Plan, India had a three-year 'plan holiday'.

28.correct: The spinal cord is protected by the vertebral column (backbone).

wrong.A: The ribs protect the heart and lungs.
wrong.C: The cerebellum is part of the brain.
wrong.D: The pons is part of the brainstem.

28.fact: The brain is protected by the skull (cranium); the spinal cord by the vertebrae.

29.correct: The Fifth Five-Year Plan (1974-79) focused on poverty alleviation ('Garibi Hatao') and employment generation.

wrong.B: Export-oriented growth was not its focus.
wrong.D: It was not about industrialization only.

29.fact: The Minimum Needs Programme was launched during the Fifth Plan.

30.correct: In Olympic-style boxing, 5 judges score a bout.

wrong.A: 3 judges is not the Olympic standard.

30.fact: Boxing uses the 10-point must system, with judges scoring each round.

31.correct: Atal Bhujal Yojana promotes the rational use of groundwater through community participation.

wrong.A: PM-Kisan provides income support to farmers.
wrong.B: The Jal Jeevan Mission focuses on tap-water supply.
wrong.D: The Atal Pension Yojana is a pension scheme.

31.fact: Atal Bhujal Yojana is a World Bank-supported scheme for sustainable groundwater management.

32.correct: Iron is commonly referred to as the 'key metal of industrial development' because it is the basis of steel and machinery.

wrong.A: Copper is important but not the 'key metal'.
wrong.C: Aluminium is important but not the key metal here.
wrong.D: Manganese is an alloying metal, not the key metal.

32.fact: Iron and steel form the backbone of construction, transport and manufacturing.

33.correct: NAMASTE stands for 'National Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem'.

wrong.B: 'New Authority for Mechanized Service Ecosystem' is incorrect.
wrong.C: 'New Action for Mechanized Sanitation Ecosystem' is incorrect.
wrong.D: 'National Authority for Mechanized Service Ecosystem' is incorrect.

33.fact: NAMASTE aims to end hazardous manual cleaning of sewers and septic tanks.

34.correct: Under the AB PM-JAY expansion, senior citizens (70+) get an additional top-up cover of up to Rs 5 lakh per year.

wrong.C: This option is garbled and incorrect.

34.fact: AB PM-JAY (Ayushman Bharat) provides health cover of Rs 5 lakh per family per year.

35.correct: Rudrasena II was a ruler of the Vakataka dynasty (of the Deccan).

wrong.A: He was not of the Nanda dynasty.
wrong.B: He was not of the Maurya dynasty.
wrong.D: He was not of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty.

35.fact: The Vakataka king Rudrasena II married Prabhavatigupta, daughter of Chandragupta II.

36.correct: India's Augmented Satellite Launch Vehicle (ASLV) had 5 stages.
36.fact: The ASLV was an experimental launcher that bridged the SLV-3 and the PSLV.

37.correct: C. Rajagopalachari became the first Indian (and last overall) Governor-General of independent India.

wrong.B: Vallabhbhai Patel was the first Home Minister/Deputy PM.
wrong.C: Rajendra Prasad became the first President.
wrong.D: Jawaharlal Nehru was the first Prime Minister.

37.fact: Lord Mountbatten was the first Governor-General after independence; Rajaji was the first Indian one.

38.correct: The proportion of the elderly (60+) population in India is projected to increase to about 23 crore by 2036.
38.fact: India's population is gradually ageing as fertility declines and life expectancy rises.

39.correct: In the 3000 m steeplechase, there are 28 hurdle jumps and 7 water jumps.
39.fact: The steeplechase combines distance running with fixed barriers and a water jump.

40.correct: Japan is NOT in the top 5 of the Global Firepower Index 2025 (the top ranks include the USA, Russia, China and India).

wrong.C: South Korea has been ranked among the top 5.

40.fact: The Global Firepower Index ranks countries by their military strength.
